strategy: Hit with Storm — he takes +30% from it — and avoid his 64% Myth resist.
Let Round 1 play out. At the end of it he loads a +100% Balanceblade, a Feint, a +200% outgoing Berserk aura, and a harmless 0-damage Tempest. The Berserk is the only real threat.
His own Supernova immediately strips that Berserk aura off him, so the big-hit danger is gone by Round 2 while his Balanceblade stays.
He is stunable — stun him if you need to skip a dangerous turn while you set up.
Stack blades over the next rounds and end it with one boosted Storm hit. His per-round Spinysaur only chips ~214 to a random player, so just stay healed.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1 he buffs himself: +100% Balanceblade, a Feint, a +200% outgoing Berserk, and a 0-damage Tempest. Only the Berserk makes his hits scary.
Right after, his own Supernova removes the Berserk aura from him, so the +200% damage threat is gone entering Round 2.
Every round he throws a small Spinysaur (~214 Life) at a random player. Minor chip damage; just keep the team topped up.
